India now turning to China for Urea & Nitrogen fertilizers. China produced 77.7mt ammonia in 2025, 52.56mt N2 purity fertilizer & 72mt Urea (up 6-7%).

9.34mt in total N2 Fertilizer export (up 68.6% YoY)
4.89mt in Urea, up 17.8x ($410/ton)
China consumed 44mt of N2 fertilizer. https://t.co/VzeB514wBu https://t.co/aKGvg22PZ1

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
Dump Trucks are also getting electrified:
2 recent stories from BYD & ChangAn.
BYD delivered 50 dump trucks to the Zhengzhou land port zone & ChangAn delivered 5 105 tone wide dump trucks to Shaanxi project for transporting mining transport.

Improved eTech is allowing more https://t.co/oXWdAwi4je

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
BYD in Brazil will build up lightning charging network w/ 1000 stations by end of 2027. Z9GT will be 1st model to support lightning charging.

It is also currently expanding its Denza operation. A 2nd store just opened up in Brasilia. By end of 2026, there will be 22 such stores. https://t.co/bh8FC9RU9A

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
There it is. BYD looking into building plant in Canada according to Bloomberg & also acquiring a more established automaker.

It's already growing really fast in global mkt, but acquiring an automaker w/ global presence would allow it to skip the long process of building up sales https://t.co/uouhqXMP56

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
CGN just grid connected 三澳/San'ao NPP unit 1 in Zhejiang, the 1st Hualong-1 NPP in Yangtze River Delta.

This is part of 6 NPP cluster, 3 are already under construction. When fully built, this cluster will generate 54 TWh of electricity per yr, enough for 5m ppl. https://t.co/0GJmaRseP2

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
Feb data from CABIA on battery sales & exports.
74.5 GWh auto battery 29% NCM & 71% LFP
38.6 GWh BESS 99.9% LFP
For exports:
16.9 GWh auto battery 54.6% NCM & 45.2% LFP
7.0 GWh BESS all LFP
-> even in CNY month, domestic mkt far bigger than export + Chinese automakers use LFP far https://t.co/FW9Uq3RtYa

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
See export by month & region. It was mostly to Asia & Latam regions, which need to import Chinese fertilizers to feed the population.

Urea Exports surged in 2nd half likely due to higher capacity + lower domestic pricing. https://t.co/L7VOQY9xTW

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
China's fertilizer/urea industry had just 1% gross margin in 2025 w/ 47% of companies losing money. So it likely would be able to produce more if the px is higher.

Top destinations were Sri Lanka, Vietnam, Mexico, Ethiopia, Chile & Malaysia. All need to feed a lot of ppl. https://t.co/bSqNyfMXtC

tweets.author: tphuang
tweets.text:
China has also been ramping up "green urea" + ammonia capacity which will come online from this yr.

So while GCC fertilizer production will be a big loss, I do think this can be mitigated by higher pricing & greater utilization from other producers
https://t.co/wlIpGMtL5d
